perturbation.original_text: 4. Disclosures Required by Law or Regulation. A Receiving Party shall not be r estricted from disclosing Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party or any Notes as required pursuant to any law, regulation or judicial or governmental order, or request of a regulatory or self-regulatory entity having examination authority over Receiving Party or a member of its Group, provided that Receiving Party will disclose only such information as it believes is required to comply with the order or request and, in the case of a court order or subpoena and to the extent legally permitted, the Receiving Party shall promptly notify the Disclosing Party and shall if reasonably practicable, cooperate with the Disclosing Party, at the Disclosing Party’s expense, so that the Disclosing Party take appropriate remedies or other appropriate protection.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Miscellaneous. This Agreement contains the entire understanding and agreement of the parties relating to the subject matter hereof. No provision of this Agreement may be amended, modified or waived except in writing signed by the party sought to be bound. No custom or course of dealing shall cause a modification of this Agr eement. The paragraph headings used herein are for convenience of reference only and will not affect the interpretation or construction of this Agreement. The failure of any party to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver of any rights or remedies available to such party or its right to subsequently enforce such provision or any other provision of this Agreement. Each party is responsible for their own costs and expenses in relation to fulfilling the aim and intention of this agreement and any meetings arising thereof. A Receiving Party shall not be r estricted from disclosing Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party or any Notes as required pursuant to any law, regulation or judicial or governmental order, or request of a regulatory or self-regulatory entity having examination authority over Receiving Party or a member of its Group, provided that Receiving Party will disclose only such information as it believes is required to comply with the order or request and, in the case of a court order or subpoena and to the extent legally permitted, the Receiving Party shall promptly notify the Disclosing Party and shall if reasonably practicable, cooperate with the Disclosing Party, at the Disclosing Party’s expense, so that the Disclosing Party take appropriate remedies or other appropriate protection.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the disclosure clause to the 'Miscellaneous' section obscures its importance and makes it less likely to be noticed by the parties involved, hindering the protection offered by mandatory disclosure laws and regulations.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 390A of the Companies Act 2006 requires companies to disclose information required by law in a clear and accessible manner. Placing a crucial disclosure about legal or regulatory requirements within the 'Miscellaneous' section makes it less accessible and visible, therefore contradicting the intended clarity and accessibility mandated by the act.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: This page is better viewed with a JavaScript enabled browser. Document not Found Document not found The document you were looking for does not exist. Click here to close this window, or click here to go back.
perturbation.justification: The original contract has a clear disclosure requirement in section 4. The perturbed contract moves this disclosure clause to section 10 'Miscellaneous.' The Perturbation Explanation indicates that this obscures the importance of the disclosure clause. The Law Explanation also states that this violates Section 390A of the Companies Act 2006, which requires companies to disclose information required by law in a clear and accessible manner. The structural flaw of moving the disclosure clause to a less conspicuous section contradicts the legal mandate for clear disclosure. While the first scraped snippet is a 'Document not found' error, the fact that the disclosure is moved makes it less accessible, thus contradicting the intent of clear disclosure as stated in the Law Explanation. Therefore, a contradiction exists.
